title: Welcome to my Mountain Bike, the "Purple Power" - Request - Stumpjumper Team with Gripshift

transcript.text: Hello, somebody wanted me to do a tour of my mountain bike, so here it is, going to me by Michael, so thank you very much.
Yeah, this is like a 20-year-old frame. It's a specialized dump jumper team.
I remember when it came out 20 years ago, the pros were racing them, and it was the best bike, so now I feel like a little kid, because I remember what bikes used to be cool.
I mean, I had one of these when I was a kid, and they work well with me.
So, it has a grid shift, 9 speed in the back, 44 teeth in the front, and 12 in the back, so it's good enough for me, my level right now.
You know, and the V-brakes, and the XT cranks, and I think some XT hubs, and it's got the goop in the wheels, so I can get the flat, and it fixes itself.
The bike's purple, the seat's purple, and the bag's purple, so I thought that was cute.
We just weighed it. It weighs 27 pounds, so that's, I mean, it could be a faulty scale.
The scale says I weigh 193 pounds, and I thought I was less, but whatever.
It's good enough. I mean, it feels light enough.
And, um, I haven't named it yet, but, uh, that's my bicycle.
And then you have some, and then you're, here's some, here's some, here's some zooming in.
See, see, see, see, see, see, see, see, see, see, see.
See, I haven't put my soul in it yet, but it already has some soul, so that's pretty cool.
